What it means: Holds the front line. Does not do much damage; its value is the utility it provides — shields, heals, crowd control, soaking.
Used by: Poppy, Galio, Jarvan IV, Kled, Swain, Sion, K'Sante, Thresh, Taric, Zac, Amumu

What it means: Fights in the front line and deals a lot of damage. A melee unit that is not built to survive is built to kill.
Used by: Garen, Darius, Gwen, Rek'Sai, Bel'Veth, Aatrox, Warwick, Urgot, Olaf, Kayn, Yone

What it means: Ranged, and deals its damage with the ABILITY itself. Every Mage is AP (the one exception is Ryze, whose damage scales on Armor + MR).
Used by: Sona, Lux, Cassiopeia, Karma, Ahri, Taliyah, Vel'Koz, Annie, Seraphine, Teemo

What it means: Ranged, and deals its damage by ATTACKING — its own auto-attacks or a summon's. USUALLY AD, but NOT a synonym for it: 7 Set 9 Marksmen are AP (see the note below).
Used by: Kayle, Samira, Jhin, Akshan, Azir, Ashe, Caitlyn, Jinx, Twitch, Aphelios, Nilah

What it means: Reaches the enemy BACKLINE. The user's definition, and it is about ACCESS, not about being melee or having a dagger.
Used by: Katarina, Zed, Yasuo, Ekko, Viego, Qiyana, Graves, Akali, Evelynn

Role: ONE role per champion — what the unit IS. It sits beside `Damage Type`, which says what it hits with; the two were a single glued value until 2026-07-21 (Set 10's source spells them together as `ADTank`, `APCaster`).

THIS COLUMN REPLACED THE IMPORTED SOURCE VALUE and is no longer refreshable from it. The source could never answer the question across both sets: Set 9 states only Carry / Tank / Support (3 values) and Set 10 states 13 AD/AP-prefixed variants, so `Role` could not be filtered, grouped or compared until it was normalised onto these five. Set 10's `HighMana` and `Crit` qualifiers were dropped in that collapse; nothing else was lost, because damage type moved into its own column rather than being discarded.

TANK vs FIGHTER IS A DAMAGE TEST, NOT A CLASS TEST (the user: 'Tank = don't do much damage but provide other utility. Fighter = do damage a lot'). Class does not predict it — K'Sante is a Bastion near the top of the damage ranking and Garen a Juggernaut near the bottom, while Maokai and Taric score zero.

ASSASSIN IS ABOUT REACHING THE BACK LINE, so a class trait cannot decide it. `Move Behind` alone is not enough — Urgot and Gnar jump into the enemy but, in the user's words, 'most of time they don't reach backline'. A CROWD DIVER IS A FIGHTER, not an assassin. The accepted consequence: Zed and Katarina are Assassins in Set 9 (Rogue) and Fighters in Set 10 (Crowd Diver) — the SET'S OWN TRAIT decides, not the character's reputation.

A RANGED UNIT CAN NEVER BE Tank / Fighter / Assassin, because all three describe fighting in or through the front line. Range is the arbiter, because it is the one fact that cannot be flavour: the melee-sounding classes are not range-pure (Slayer, Executioner, Challenger, Vanquisher and Edgelord all contain ranged champions, which once made Set 9 Kayle an 'AOE Assassin' at range 4).

`Support` USED TO BE A SIXTH ROLE AND WAS REMOVED — of the 11 champions carrying it, only 5 targeted an ally at all; the rest (Ashe, Heimerdinger, Lissandra, Milio) were ranged damage dealers with a status effect. What they actually provide is now the `Utility` archetype.

THE ASSIGNMENTS ARE A HUMAN CALL, not a formula, and VALIDATE can only enforce the spelling. Four champions are stated flatly against the source, which calls the first three tanks and Nilah a melee carry: Warwick (S9) Fighter, Urgot (BOTH sets) Fighter, Olaf (S10) Fighter, Nilah (S9) Marksman.

MAGE vs MARKSMAN IS NOT THE SAME QUESTION AS 'Damage Type', AND THE TWO DISAGREE FOR 7 CHAMPIONS - all in Set 9, all AP but classed Marksman: Kayle, Azir, Kalista, Kai'Sa, Heimerdinger, Miss Fortune, Gangplank. The reason is historical: the split was derived per set from whatever that set stated, and the two sets state different things. Set 10's source names the damage type outright (ADCaster / APCaster), so its split follows damage. Set 9's source does not, so its split fell back on the CLASS trait - and none of those 7 carries a caster class (Slayer, Strategist, Challenger, Gunner). They are AP-scaling units that fight by attacking, which is a real thing to be; the roles were reviewed and signed off as they stand. FLAGGED, NOT SILENTLY CHANGED - if the intent is that Marksman means AD, these 7 are the rows to move.

GANGPLANK IS RANGE 1 - MELEE - AND STILL A MARKSMAN, which contradicts the rule above that a ranged unit and a front-line unit are different things. He reached Marksman through his Gunner class rather than through his range. Nilah is the deliberate version of this (range 2, the user's explicit call); Gangplank was never ruled on. FLAGGED.
